objective: Add a repo-native lane-claim gate so agents cannot start implementation work in a different issue lane without a matching execution-scope claim.
scope_boundary: Extend the existing execution-scope validator with an optional lane_claim contract.
Wire lane-claim enforcement into execution-environment preflight and the governance Local CI profile.
Document the rule and preserve focused regression coverage.
out_of_scope: Cleaning or removing the accidentally created issue #391 worktree.
Changing GitHub org or repository settings.
Creating a separate lock service or durable external lane registry.
research_summary: The best repo-native enforcement point is the existing execution-scope contract. It already owns root, branch, write surface, forbidden roots, active parallel roots, and handoff evidence. Adding lane_claim there avoids a second source of truth while letting callers opt into hard lane ownership enforcement.

sprints.name: Lane-claim execution-scope gate
sprints.goal: Fail closed when implementation work lacks an explicit lane claim matching the current issue branch.
tasks: Add lane_claim parsing and enforcement to assert_execution_scope.py.
Require lane claims in the governance Local CI planner-boundary command.
Make Local CI scope resolution validate lane_claim.issue_number.
Run focused unit tests, governance validators, execution-scope validation, and Local CI.
acceptance_criteria: A mismatched branch/scope issue fails with --require-lane-claim.
Local CI refuses an issue scope whose filename matches but lane_claim.issue_number does not.
The #393 execution scope passes lane-claim enforcement.

specialist_reviews.focus: Find the best existing enforcement point for lane ownership without creating a drifting parallel lock system.
specialist_reviews.summary: Recommended embedding lane_claim in execution scopes and enforcing it via assert_execution_scope.py, check_execution_environment.py, and Local CI Gate.

alternate_model_review.summary: A full cross-family review should be added before broad rollout to downstream repos; this governance-local guard is still safe because it only tightens existing local/CI checks.

execution_handoff.approved_scope_summary: Issue #393 may update execution-scope validators, Local CI gate wiring, standards/registry docs, and issue-specific planning/scope artifacts.
execution_handoff.next_execution_step: Implement lane_claim enforcement and validate with focused tests plus Local CI.

material_deviation_rules: Do not create a new external lock service for this slice.
Do not mutate issue #391 or its worktree as part of this guardrail implementation.
If lane ownership cannot be determined from execution scope, fail closed when enforcement is requested.
